thinking.text:
This is getting quite complex. Let me think about a better approach.

The game summary says 262 moves were made. My current solution has 127 commands (after cleaning). I'm missing about half the commands because:
1. The 1fps frame rate misses commands typed between frames
2. My tracking of the newest completed command might miss some

Let me re-examine the video more carefully. Perhaps I should look at the 5fps frames that we already have OCR'd (93 files) and also try to get more.

Actually, let me re-examine the problem. The task says "Transcribe the entire contents of the text, and create a file /app/solution.txt that has all the moves they input, one per line, in the format 'n' or 'get bag' etc."

So I need to extract ALL commands the player typed. Let me look at the game summary again: 262 moves. My current extraction has 127 unique commands (after fixes). This is because the tracking script only captures when the "newest completed command" changes, but at 1fps, multiple commands can be completed between frames.

I need to look at the ACTUAL transcript in each frame more carefully. Each frame shows a list of commands in the transcript. When I compare frame N to frame N+1, NEW commands appear at the bottom (or top) of the transcript. I should look at ALL commands that appear new in each frame, not just the newest one.

Let me write a script that:
1. For each frame, extracts ALL commands from the transcript (excluding the typing line)
2. Compares with the previous frame to find which commands are NEW
3. Adds those new commands to the sequence

This should capture all commands, even if multiple are typed between frames.
